etymology.description: 金(金) (jīn, "gold; metal"), 字(字) (zì, "character; word"), 塔(塔) (tǎ, "tower; pagoda")
etymology.interpretation: The term 金字塔 (jīnzìtǎ) refers to the shape of a pyramid, which resembles a 金(金) (jīn, "gold") character (字) when viewed from a certain angle, and 塔(塔) (tǎ, "tower"), suggesting a tall, monumental structure like a pyramid. The word conveys the shape and grandness of pyramids, akin to pagodas in their towering appearance.

examples.chinese: 埃及的金字塔(埃及的金字塔)是古代建筑的奇迹。
examples.pinyin: Āijí de jīnzìtǎ shì gǔdài jiànzhú de qíjì.
examples.english: The pyramids of Egypt are wonders of ancient architecture.

examples.chinese: 金字塔(金字塔)在沙漠中显得格外壮观。
examples.pinyin: Jīnzìtǎ zài shāmò zhōng xiǎnde géwài zhuàngguān.
examples.english: The pyramids appear particularly magnificent in the desert.

examples.chinese: 旅游者们(旅遊者們)纷纷去参观(去參觀)金字塔(金字塔)。
examples.english: Tourists flock to visit the pyramids.
